  "findings": [
    "Sidewall pressure and/or jamming limits exceeded during wire pull, indicating defective installation planning/execution.",
    "Conductors may be damaged (insulation compression, deformation, or jacket damage) due to excessive pulling forces.",
    "Identification of grounded/neutral and phase conductors cannot be verified as compliant until conductors are inspected for damage."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the pull immediately and relieve tension.",
    "Remove and inspect the affected conductors for damage; replace any damaged conductors.",
    "Recalculate maximum allowable pulling tension, sidewall pressure, and jam ratio for the raceway layout.",
    "Verify raceway size, bend radii, number of bends, and pull-box spacing are suitable before re-pulling.",
    "Use appropriate pulling lubricant, tension monitoring, and cable-pulling equipment.",
    "Re-pull conductors within calculated limits and inspect before continuing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Exceeding sidewall pressure or jamming limits during a wire pull is a sequence and compliance violation. The pull must be stopped, damaged conductors removed/inspected, and the installation recalculated before re-pulling. Continuing risks conductor damage, insulation failure, and code non-compliance.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 200.6",
    "NEC 210.5"
  ]
}
